topic: Phase A2 accuracy-math sidebar demotion — verify re-inserted self-serving-denominator sentence survived round-trip and proposal is adversarially sound
our_argument_summary: EXP-211-redesign proposes consolidating the two Overview accuracy-math callouts (hero 4+4 sc-card sublabel arithmetic clause + bordered accent box) into a single <aside class="accuracy-math-aside"> placed after the sc-breakdown block and before Downloads. The aside is pitched at 0.92rem with a 3px var(--rule) left border, no background. Per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001 (operator, 2026-04-23T03:20Z), the sentence "The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator." was re-inserted after the curmudgeon c1 review flagged its absence as major (ISS-1437). This review verifies that re-insertion.

operator_verification.description: HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001 asked the curmudgeon to verify three conditions on the re-inserted sentence.

checks.requirement: Sentence is present in EXP-211-redesign aside (ama-col-source paragraph 1, before Section 6.6 link)
checks.evidence: content_diff.change_3_insert_accuracy_math_aside.new_aside_html contains the sentence at char-index 1600, after the ama-col-source div open (char-index 1095) and before the #part6 Section 6.6 anchor link (char-index 1740). Placement matches the operator spec exactly.

checks.requirement: Wording preserves the load-bearing words "self-serving" and "manually entered"
checks.evidence: Sentence reads verbatim: "The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator." Both load-bearing words present, unmodified. No paraphrase or softening.

checks.requirement: Sentence reads naturally in context and does not undercut the aside's lower-pitch design intent
checks.evidence: Flow inside the ama-col-source paragraph is: (1) factual lead — "The headline is not computed by any script"; (2) mechanism — "static string in HTML source"; (3) negative enumeration — "No Python script, no JavaScript..."; (4) evidence — "arithmetic stated on wins page but no script validates against registry"; (5) EDITORIAL SUMMARY — "manually entered with a self-serving denominator"; (6) detail link — Section 6.6. The editorial sentence sits as an earned conclusion on top of four sentences of factual substrate, not as a standalone pronouncement. Lower-pitch design intent is about VISUAL weight (font-size, border treatment, muted color) not semantic restraint; re-inserting an editorial sentence into a visually demoted container does not re-inflate pitch. The restraint principle that drove the original drop — "do not pre-load verdicts in chrome" — applies to chrome-pitch callouts (hero, bordered accent box), not to a muted aside where the editorial sentence reads as the end of a factual paragraph, not as chrome.

operator_verification.overall_verification: All three operator conditions satisfied. Sentence has cleanly round-tripped through the EXP draft — placement, wording, and naturalness of reading all confirmed. Decider can proceed with Phase A2 integration when operator posts the approval HNOTE.
operator_verification.counterpoint_considered: From a strict "credibility-through-restraint" reading (the analyst's original position), a defender of the original drop could argue: the factual substrate already lets the reader reach the "self-serving" conclusion on their own, and stating it explicitly weakens that rhetorical effect. That is a real tradeoff, but the operator's override is sound — a reader scanning a muted aside is unlikely to stop, reconstruct the denominator arithmetic, and independently coin the phrase "self-serving." The explicit summary sentence is what lets a skim-reader take home the critique. Layout demotion and editorial clarity are orthogonal; the operator correctly refused to let one eat the other.

holes_found.description: Defensive-rendering ternary wrapping inconsistency. anticipated_objections[5] notes that ama-variant must be wrapped in a conditional ${accuracyVariantList ? ... : ""} to avoid a double-space grammar bug when variantSources is empty, and the removed_prose_items_note flags this as an IMPLEMENTATION NOTE for the decider. BUT change_3.new_aside_html still shows the UNCONDITIONAL form of the ama-variant paragraph. A decider integrating this EXP who reads only the new_aside_html template (the natural integration target) will ship the unwrapped version and introduce the grammar bug whenever accuracyVariantList is empty. Two places disagree with each other inside the same EXP file.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) update change_3.new_aside_html to show the ternary-wrapped form inline so the integration target is self-consistent, or (b) add a loud, placed warning inside change_3 itself (not only in anticipated_objections further down) pointing the decider at the ternary requirement. The current arrangement relies on the decider reading anticipated_objections[5] in full, which is a fragile assumption for a multi-hundred-line EXP.

holes_found.description: content_preservation_audit.preserved_source_code_critique_clauses claims three sentences are preserved "verbatim" but introduces small word-level changes that do not affect meaning: (1) "static string in the HTML source code" → "static string in the HTML source" (drops "code"); (2) "below-detection-threshold entries" → "below-detection entries" (drops "-threshold") in the ama-variant paragraph phrasing; (3) "When the model's own internal data is queried, it returns X" → "Internal queries return X" (condensed). These are editorial polish, not content loss, but the audit's explicit use of the word "verbatim" overstates the preservation. An adversarial dome defender could point to the audit's claim as overreach.
holes_found.recommendation: Downgrade the audit's "verbatim" language to "near-verbatim (minor punctuation and wording polish, no clause dropped)" — the substance-preservation claim stands but the word "verbatim" promises more than the diff delivers.

holes_found.description: The kicker text "Accuracy math — detail for the calibrated reader" is self-flagged by the analyst (notes_for_human_review_group[0], anticipated_objections[2]) as editorializing about the audience. "Calibrated reader" reads as a compliment to the persistent reader and an implicit put-down of the skim-reader; it arguably violates the brief's restraint direction in the one surface element (the kicker) that is styled to look like metadata rather than prose. The analyst's own fallback recommendation is "Accuracy math — supporting detail," which is cleaner.
holes_found.recommendation: Adopt the analyst's second-choice default: "Accuracy math — supporting detail." Two-word change; no functional impact. If the human-review group prefers the editorialized form, they can override — but the default should be the purely descriptive phrasing.

holes_found.description: CC2 acceptance criterion ("one-scroll reachability from hero card") is specified as a requirement but is untestable at spec-review time — it depends on actual rendered layout on a 1080p viewport with the full post-integration Overview (including EXP-209 verdict-distribution chart and the Latest Findings block). The spec hedges: "If the bar chart (EXP-209) pushes the aside out of the first scroll, the aside's first arithmetic line must still be reachable within two scrolls — measure." This is a squishy fallback that could allow the aside to slide to three or four scrolls in practice if the Latest Findings block grows, at which point the hero-to-aside adjacency that preserves CC2 is lost. Without a Playwright screenshot + viewport-scroll measurement actually run against a build, "reachability within 1-2 scrolls" is aspirational.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a concrete, measurable acceptance criterion: "On the merged 1080p rendering (with all Phase A1 EXPs plus this EXP integrated), the top edge of the accuracy-math aside sits at scrollY <= 2 * viewport_height relative to the Overview tab top." Require a Playwright screenshot + DOM.getBoundingClientRect() measurement captured at integration time, not deferred to post-integration review. If the two-scroll reachability turns out to fail in practice, the decider needs the option to adjust placement before merging, not after.

stronger_arguments.description: The aside's source-code-critique paragraph now ties the hardcoded-number pattern to the Timestamp Error tab via the new #timestamp-error cross-reference (ama-cross paragraph). This is actually a STRENGTHENING the original bordered box did not have. The curmudgeon recommends explicitly calling this out as an argumentative gain in the EXP's kernel_of_truth or preserved_load_bearing_content list — the current framing treats it as a neutral addition, but it's a net gain for the Timestamp Error tab's cross-referencing surface.

The demotion-of-pitch argument is quantitatively defensible: body .92rem vs page ~1rem (~8% size reduction), 3px var(--rule) vs 2px full-accent border (subjectively quieter), no background color vs full card-bg. Consider strengthening anticipated_objections[0] by adding these numerical deltas directly — "border weight -33%, font-size -8%, background removed" reads more like a measured demotion than "visually quieter."

kernel_of_truth.description: The external reviewer was correct that the original Overview surfaced the accuracy percentage at thesis-adjacent pitch via two independent callouts (hero sc-sublabel arithmetic + bordered accent box), and a skim-reader could reasonably leave with "89.6%, not 94.5%" as the dominant remembered number rather than the structural thesis. The EXP's consolidation into one muted aside is the right architectural move. The re-insertion of "self-serving denominator" per operator HNOTE rescues the critique's sharpness without re-inflating the visual pitch — a narrow win condition that the EXP now satisfies.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — this is a design EXP, not a WIN verdict. The kernel-of-truth framing here applies to the demotion rationale itself: the reviewer's critique (accuracy math was over-pitched) is legitimate, and the EXP responds to it correctly without sacrificing content.

recommended_edits: H1 (minor): Update change_3.new_aside_html to show the ternary-wrapped ama-variant form inline, OR add a loud implementation warning in change_3 itself pointing to the ternary requirement. Current arrangement risks the decider shipping the unwrapped version.
H2 (minor): Soften content_preservation_audit.preserved_source_code_critique_clauses language from "verbatim" to "near-verbatim (minor wording polish, no clause dropped)."

H3 (minor): Change the kicker default from "Accuracy math — detail for the calibrated reader" to "Accuracy math — supporting detail" (the analyst's second-choice option). Human-review group can override.

H4 (moderate): Strengthen CC2 acceptance criterion from narrative "reachable within 1-2 scrolls" to a concrete scrollY <= 2*viewport_height measurement with a Playwright check run at integration time.
deeper_analysis: This review has two layers: (1) a narrow operator verification of the re-inserted sentence, which is the explicit trigger for queue_id 240, and (2) a broader adversarial review of the proposal as a design-refresh EXP package. On layer (1), all three operator checks pass cleanly — the sentence is where specified, wording preserves both load-bearing terms, and reading flows naturally from factual substrate into editorial summary into detail link. No corrective action needed for the re-insertion itself. On layer (2), the proposal is substantially sound: content preservation is high (all source-code critique clauses retained), computed-counts rule (NC5) is honored, accessibility has been considered even if deferred, and the brief's pitch-demotion goal is met without sacrificing argumentative force. Four minor/moderate holes surface: (H1) ternary-wrapping inconsistency between change_3 template and anticipated_objections[5] — fixable with a one-line template edit; (H2) over-strong "verbatim" claim in the content-preservation audit — fixable with softer language; (H3) editorializing kicker phrasing flagged by the analyst themselves — adopt the second-choice default; (H4) CC2 one-scroll reachability claim is currently aspirational rather than measured — tighten the acceptance criterion. None of these block Phase A2 integration, but all four are worth patching before the EXP ships to keep the review-trail clean. The recommended action is minor_edit: fix H1 in the EXP file before decider integrates, accept or fix H2/H3 at integration time, and upgrade H4 to a measured test at integration time. Counterpoint considered: a defender of the original "drop self-serving" position could argue that the operator's re-insertion violates the brief's restraint direction. I weighed this explicitly in operator_verification.counterpoint_considered and concluded the operator's judgment is sound — layout demotion and editorial clarity are orthogonal dimensions, and refusing to let one eat the other is exactly the principle the curmudgeon-rubric HNOTE was written to enforce.
